title: investigate: stranded_prefetch_request_is_drained_before_reuse #[ignore] fails on clean HEAD
description: Characterized: NOT a clean deterministic driver bug. The test fails 3/3 in ISOLATION on a freshly-restarted container, but PASSES inside the full 'cargo test --include-ignored --test-threads=1' suite [206 passed / 1 failed, and stranded was NOT the failure there]. So it has an ORDERING/STATE dependency: it needs prior tests' connection/container warmup to pass, and the cold-connection isolation case fails the stranded-drain reuse assertion [reuse.cell(0,0) != 7+5]. Either a cold-connection stranded-prefetch-drain edge OR a test-setup assumption [the test issues a raw fetch_rows_request + abandons it; on a cold connection the drain-before-reuse may behave differently]. Same #[ignore]-live-test reliability class as f0ad. Needs focused investigation with the live-lane work below; low priority [not a normal-operation path; the borrowed/owned fetch + conformance are all clean].
close_reason: ROOT-CAUSED to a REAL driver robustness defect (the earlier 'NOT a clean deterministic driver bug / needs in-suite warmup' conclusion was wrong; it fails deterministically both isolated AND in-suite). A bare fetch_rows_request whose paired fetch_rows_ref_response is never consumed leaves the recovery phase InFlight with a stranded page on the wire and NO CancelDrainGuard armed; the next op's ensure_clean_before_request hit begin_pending_drain(InFlight) -> hard Err('operation attempted while a response is still in flight'), wedging the connection permanently (every later op also errors). FIX: ensure_clean_before_request now treats InFlight like a dropped fetch (mark_break_required -> break+drain) so the next op reclaims the wire; only reachable from the start of the next op, and fetch_rows_ref_response (the sole legit InFlight reader) never calls it, so all 30 callers are safe. Offline regression ensure_clean_drains_inflight_bare_request_before_reuse (loopback break/drain choreography; red-green verified). Verified: live test 5/5 green isolated + in-suite.

close_reason: Implemented: additive zero-copy borrowed fetch path (QueryValueRef<'buf>, BorrowedRowBatch, parse_query_response_borrowed, Connection::for_each_row_ref). Measured 90.9% fewer allocations (11.00 -> 1.01 allocs/row) + ~37% faster on a 5000-row wide batch. Owned path untouched, no unsafe, parity sentinels exact (1100 57p/5s, 2200 39p, 8000 82p).

comments.author: durakovic
comments.text:
Done on branch 0ta-borrowed-fetch (7 commits, master base). ADDITIVE zero-copy borrowed fetch path alongside the untouched owned QueryValue path.

QueryValueRef<'buf> (Copy, <=32B): Text(&str)/Raw(&[u8]) borrow the wire buffer, Number(&str) borrows a per-row arena, Boolean/Interval/DateTime are Copy, cold/non-borrowable fall back to Owned(&QueryValue); to_owned_value() -> QueryValue. TtcReader::read_bytes_borrowed/skip_bytes_field (zero-copy reads). BorrowedRowBatch::for_each_row_ref (owns buffer+cols+offsets, callback cannot escape -> sound; two-pass-per-row arena; handles duplicate bit-vector columns + page-boundary seed). parse_query_response_borrowed/BorrowedFetchResult (framing-aware; skip_row_data advances allocation-free for hot scalars; handles DESCRIBE_INFO/IMPLICIT_RESULTSET). Connection::for_each_row_ref/fetch_rows_ref. decode_number_text_into writes NUMBER text straight into the arena.

MEASURED (5000 rows x 4 cols, allocation-counter): owned 11.00 allocs/row ~15ms; borrowed 1.01 allocs/row ~9ms => 90.9% fewer allocations, ~37% faster, 21% fewer bytes. First honest profile showed borrowed WORSE (18/row); fixed via zero-alloc skip + arena-direct number decode + scratch reuse.

SOUNDNESS: no unsafe in new code, #![forbid(unsafe_code)] intact (allocation-counter dev-dep contains the unsafe). No self-referential struct / lifetime transmute; arenas never grown while borrowed.

GATE green: fmt --check, clippy --workspace -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (182 protocol tests). PARITY SENTINELS EXACT (shim rebuilt, path unchanged): test_1100 57p/5s, test_2200_number_var 39p, test_8000_dataframe 82p. Unblocks bead xad (decode offload).

title: BUG P1: call_timeout sends BREAK but does not drain server response -> reused connection reads stale packets (8 sites; parity divergence from protocol.pyx drain+reset)
close_reason: Fixed in commit c518224 (branch fix-timeout-drain). All 8 *_call_timeout arms + ping_with_timeout now break_and_drain: send BREAK, then discard in-flight DATA responses until the break-ack MARKER, run the RESET dance, and consume the trailing ORA-01013 error packet — leaving the wire clean and the connection reusable (mirrors protocol.pyx _break_external/_receive_packet/_reset 449-451,507-557). Classification: Error::CallTimeout is no longer is_connection_lost() (now is_transient) to match python-oracledb DPY-4024 keeping the session alive; ConnectionClosed is the connection-lost variant. Regression tests (before=fail proven via drain-skip mutation + bare-break contrast, after=pass): hermetic break_and_drain unit tests + live tests/reuse_after_call_timeout.rs (2s sleep / 500ms timeout then select 7+5=12 on same conn). title: [security] Pre-E6 cassette fixture in git history carries auth-era O5LOGON material
description:
The committed select_7_plus_5.tns-cassette BEFORE the E6 commit (still reachable in git history) contains real auth-era O5LOGON fields from a recorded session: AUTH_PASSWORD, AUTH_SESSKEY, AUTH_PBKDF2_SPEEDY_KEY, AUTH_PBKDF2_CSK_SALT, plus localhost:1526/FREEPDB1 and user pythontest.

SEVERITY: LOW. The creds are 'pythontest' (python-oracledb's well-known PUBLIC test account) against a local ephemeral container; the AUTH_* values are session-specific material DERIVED from that public password (not a plaintext leak, not reusable). Actual secret exposure is effectively nil.

FIXED FORWARD by E6: the working-tree fixture is now a 913B synthetic transcript (HOST=fixture-host, SERVICE_NAME=SYNTHETIC, no AUTH_* fields), and a CI-gating leak scanner (committed_fixture_manifest_and_leak_scan_pass) now fails any future fixture carrying these field names or high-entropy tokens. So recurrence is blocked.

REMAINING DECISION (USER-ONLY, destructive): whether to rewrite git history to purge the old blob. Options: (a) ACCEPT as-is given negligible sensitivity (public test creds, local DB, derived values); or (b) scrub via git-filter-repo/BFG + force-push — DESTRUCTIVE, rewrites public history, requires explicit user authorization per AGENTS.md 'Irreversible git' rule. Do NOT scrub unilaterally.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (user decision, no documentation). Exposure is effectively nil: public pythontest test creds, session-specific non-reusable O5LOGON-derived material, a local ephemeral container that no longer exists; already fixed-forward (synthetic working-tree fixture) + a CI leak-scanner blocks recurrence. A git-history scrub would rewrite all public SHAs and invalidate the RC b4a0cd3 + the W4-T2 qualification for zero real benefit, so it is NOT done. End-to-end testing confirmed clean (conformance 2578=2578 0 regressions; live lane green).

title: Fix test_1600 test_1612 DML-RETURNING ORA-12899 client wire deadlock (bead zhm)
close_reason: FIXED on branch fix-hangs (corresponds to bead zhm filed in d49 lane commit 9cdc28d; that lane's beads DB not merged here, so tracked as 3mr). Root cause (live strace + faulthandler proof): on the INSERT...RETURNING ORA-12899 error path the server reports the error out-of-band via a BREAK marker; client runs RESET dance, then server sends a FLUSH_OUT_BINDS request (DATA packet, data-flags 0x0000, payload ending in msg byte 0x13). Our read_data_response_boundary re-fed that post-reset packet through data_packet_ends_response, which returns false for a flagless packet that merely ends in 0x13 (correct for the n2s wide-row guard) -> looped reading a packet the server never sends (it awaited our FLUSH_OUT_BINDS reply) -> epoll deadlock 180s. Fix (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs): after the boundary loop runs a RESET, treat a packet whose payload ends in FLUSH_OUT_BINDS/END_OF_RESPONSE as the boundary (reference message-byte framing for post-reset packets), gated on post-reset context so the wide-row guard is untouched. Before=hang(180s timeout); after=test_1612 0.33s, full test_1600 27p/4.69s = reference 27p. Hermetic regression test dml_returning_error_flush_out_binds_after_reset_completes_without_hang added (hangs->times-out pre-fix, passes post-fix). Gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, cargo test --workspace (oracledb 71p, protocol 222p). NOTE on test_4100: re-checked, the historical suspected hang does NOT reproduce at this HEAD (27p/4.29s) - already fixed by earlier waves (commit e0c636c ThinVar self-deadlock fix); NOT a distinct bug, no new bead warranted.

title: [W2-T2] Modernize README Quickstart to the four-family API (Rows, not QueryResultExt)
description:
WHY: The crates.io/GitHub front-page README Quickstart (Async example, Binds & named parameters, Typed rows with #[derive(FromRow)]) still demonstrates the PRE-four-family API: it calls conn.query(...).await? and then result.get(0,0) via QueryResultExt, plus deprecated query_named and result.rows_as::<T>(). After W1-T3, async/blocking query returns Rows<'conn> (not QueryResult), so QueryResultExt::get(row,col)/get_by_name/rows_as do NOT resolve on it — these README snippets will not compile against 0.3.0. This was missed when the four-family API landed (W1-T3) and is a real front-page defect for the 0.3.0 migration release.

SCOPE: Rewrite README.md Quickstart code blocks to the CURRENT four-family API, mirroring the doc-tested forms in docs/MIGRATING-0.3.md:
 - query(cx, sql, params).await? -> Rows; .collect(cx).await? -> Vec<Row>; Row::get::<T>(col)/get by &str name. For a single scalar use query_one -> Row then row.get::<T>(0).
 - query_named(...) -> query(cx, sql, params!{ ":id" => .. }) (named binds via params! macro; query_named is #[deprecated]).
 - Typed rows: Rows::into_typed::<T>() (T: FromRow) replaces QueryResultExt::rows_as::<T>().
 - Verify the top 'Quick example' and blocking quickstart blocks are also current.
 - grep the whole README for any other deprecated/removed-shape API usage and fix.

ACCEPTANCE: No README code block uses QueryResultExt::get/get_by_name/rows_as on a query() result, no deprecated method names appear in README examples, and every rewritten snippet type-checks against the real API.

TESTS: Prove each rewritten snippet compiles by assembling them into a temporary scratch compile target (e.g. a no_run async fn body) and building it; remove the scratch artifact afterward. Mirror docs/MIGRATING-0.3.md (those forms are doc-tested and pass).

DEPS: discovered-from W2-T1.1; blocks W2-T1.4 (publish 0.3.0) — a quality 0.3.0 must not ship a non-compiling README.
close_reason: Done: README Quickstart modernized to four-family API (Rows/collect/Row::get/query_one/into_typed/params!); compile-proven; verified.

title: investigate: borrowed_fetch_handles_multipage_clob_lob_prefetch fails on clean HEAD (CLOB DefineMetadata size=0)
description: REAL DRIVER BUG, two layers (deterministic; verified via instrumentation). The borrowed zero-copy for_each_row_ref returns size=0/chunk_size=0 for CLOB/BLOB on ALL rows (not size-threshold: a 200-char CLOB also fails). LAYER 1 [registration gap]: for_each_row_ref opens via execute_query_with_bind_rows_and_options_core [lib.rs ~4029], which does NOT register the cursor in self.lob_prefetch_cursors; only the higher-level execute_query_with_bind_rows_options_adjusted [lib.rs ~3539-3541] does. Instrumentation: DBG showed cursor_id=1, cols=2, has_lob=true, contains=FALSE. Also the _adjusted/_core condition gates on !rows.is_empty(), but a CLOB query's first execute returns the describe with ZERO inline rows [LOB columns force per-row define-fetch], so registration must gate on columns_have_lob_prefetch_fields alone [like define_and_fetch lib.rs:4142]. FIX 1: register in for_each_row_ref after open, columns-only [verified: makes contains=true]. LAYER 2 [borrowed DefineMetadata decode drops size]: even after FIX 1 makes the paged fetch route through parse_define_fetch_response_borrowed [LobDecodeMode::DefineMetadata, fetch.rs], the decoded lob.size/chunk_size are STILL 0. So the borrowed DefineMetadata LOB decode [parse_column_slot/parse_lob_value DefineMetadata path] does not read the prefetched size[ub8]/chunk_size[ub4] from the wire. Compare to the OWNED DefineMetadata decode [parse_lob_value owned] + the python-oracledb reference [decoders.pyx LOB + base.pyx LOB-prefetch read]. NOT data corruption [the locator is valid; read_lob works]. Partial WIP stashed [stash 'bur7-wip-partial-diagnosis']. Needs a focused fix of BOTH layers with the test as the regression + conformance 0-regressions [LOB decode is sensitive].
close_reason: FIXED [b11a60c]. Two layers: (1) for_each_row_ref now registers CLOB/BLOB cursors in lob_prefetch_cursors right after the initial execute, gated on columns alone [lib.rs ~4058], so paged borrowed fetches route to DefineMetadata. (2) The real second bug was request/parser PAIRING, not the decoder: LOB-prefetch cursors were parsing responses as DefineMetadata but SENDING a plain fetch payload [so the server never returned the prefetch size/chunk_size]. Now all three fetch paths [owned 3775, direct borrowed 3845, pipelined borrowed 3928] send build_define_fetch_payload when lob_prefetch_cursors.contains(cursor_id) [the same gate as the parse selection at 3987]. Reference-grounded: python-oracledb base.pyx:1395-1428/1074-1080 + packet.pyx:484-491 [ub4 num_bytes, ub8 size, ub4 chunk_size, locator]. VERIFIED: regression test 2/2 x3 runs deterministic; conformance 2578=2578 0 regressions 0 missing; full driver live suite 0 failures; fmt/clippy/gen_baseline[public_api unchanged] green. NOT data corruption was the original framing; this restores the borrowed LOB prefetch optimization + correct inline size/chunk_size metadata.

Reference (messages/auth.pyx _write_message + crypto.pyx get_signature): when private_key is present, also write AUTH_HEADER (date/(request-target)/host) + AUTH_SIGNATURE (base64(RSA PKCS1v15 SHA256 sign of the header)) and OR TNS_AUTH_MODE_IAM_TOKEN into auth_mode.

Low priority unless oraclemcp needs instance/resource-principal signing rather than a db-token string.

title: [tracked-exception] connect-string / HA transport feature gaps (DSN params, failover, REDIRECT, Oracle-SNI)
description:
E8 Round-2 Pass F. Four connect/transport FEATURE-COMPLETENESS gaps (NOT data-corruption; conformance green; most fail-closed). TRACKED TECHNICAL EXCEPTION per E8 acceptance, to be addressed in a dedicated transport-hardening effort (post-1.0 or a W4 item); SUPPORT.md to document them as known limitations so they are explicit, not silent.

F1 (lib.rs:3494) connect uses hard-coded Duration::from_secs(20) + descriptor host/port only; parsed DSN DESCRIPTION/EZConnect-Plus params (transport_connect_timeout, sdu, security, wallet_location, use_sni) are NOT applied. Footgun: a user-set DSN timeout is silently ignored.
F2 (net/mod.rs:58) multi-address ADDRESS_LIST is flattened to the first usable address; no failover/load-balance to subsequent addresses.
F3 (tls.rs:406) use_sni=true silently degrades to enable_sni=false (placeholder oracle.invalid) because Oracle SNI is not a rustls-valid DNS name; should fail-closed or do a real Oracle-SNI handshake when SNI is explicitly requested.
F4 (lib.rs:3553) listener REDIRECT (RAC/SCAN handoff) is a hard Error::RedirectUnsupported; no redirect-follow.

Scope note: E8 core is protocol/codec/multi-packet/async correctness; these are connection-management features. Disposition: document in SUPPORT.md + this tracked exception; fix in a focused transport epic. F1's silent-ignore should at minimum be documented; consider rejecting unsupported DSN params rather than silently ignoring.

title: transient 'unknown TTC message type 129' under default-parallel live tests on small container
description: Confirmed environment/contention-sensitive [NOT a code defect]. live_typed_roundtrip_matrix_e12 passes 3/3 in ISOLATION but intermittently fails inside the full suite [observed under both default-parallel load AND once in a serial --include-ignored run]. The driver itself is correct [conformance 2578=2578 0 regressions; full borrowed+owned fetch clean]. Root pattern: the #[ignore] live tests run against a single small FREEPDB1 dev container and are sensitive to load + container/session state + test ordering. BROADER 1.0 ACTION: provision a properly-resourced DB and wire a LIVE-TEST LANE into CI [the #[ignore] tests are currently outside the gate, so they accumulate context-dependent failures AND real regressions go uncaught — bur7 was a real bug that sat undetected precisely because its test is #[ignore]]. Then make the contention-sensitive tests robust [controlled isolation / retries].
close_reason: ROOT-CAUSED to a REAL driver bug (the earlier 'environment/contention-sensitive, NOT a code defect' conclusion was wrong). The matrix re-executes a cached cursor (select :1 as v from dual) whose column type changed CHAR->CLOB; the server re-describes it as CLOB but streams the value in LONG form (adjust_refetch_metadata folds CLOB->LONG), which carries the LONG status trailer (null-indicator sb4 + return-code ub4 = ORA-01405). The EXECUTE parse path runs with fetch_long_status=false, so the 5-byte trailer was left unconsumed and the next message byte 0x81 was mis-read as 'unknown TTC message type 129 at position 95'. Intermittent because the adjust only fires when the cold server re-describes mid-response. FIX: fetch.rs promotes fetch_long_status when adjust_refetch_metadata folds a column to LONG/LONG RAW, so parse_row_data consumes the trailer (covers CLOB->LONG and BLOB->LONG RAW). Offline regression query_response_consumes_long_trailer_for_clob_refetched_as_long (real 23ai wire capture; red-green verified). Verified: cold-bootstrap repro 0/18 fail post-fix (was 1/1); full live lane 5/5 green with the test re-included.

title: [bug] query_one/query_opt falsely return TooManyRows for single-row LONG (and any arraysize=1) results
description:
FOUND BY W3-E1.2 live typed matrix. query_one / query_opt over a LONG (LONG RAW) column return Error::TooManyRows even when the query yields exactly one row; collecting the same query returns one row with the correct value.

ROOT CAUSE (lib.rs:2463-2479, Rows::one()/opt()): the cardinality test is
    let too_many = self.more_rows || self.batch.len() > 1;
But more_rows=true means 'server has NOT confirmed end-of-data', not '>1 row'. For normal columns the default arraysize (lib.rs:1621, prefetch=arraysize) is large, so a 1-row result returns more_rows=false and one() works. LONG/LONG RAW require a per-row define-fetch round trip (statement._requires_define, lib.rs:7082/9908), so the fetch is effectively single-row and the server returns 1 row with more_rows=true -> one()/opt() misread that as TooManyRows. The same defect hits any query where arraysize/prefetch is 1 (LONG/LOB, or user-set arraysize(1)).

FIX: do not treat more_rows as definitive cardinality. one()/opt() must confirm: if exactly one row is in hand and more_rows is set, fetch the next batch; only TooManyRows if a second row actually materializes (batch.len() reaches 2 or a follow-up batch is non-empty). Bounded to at most one extra fetch in the arraysize=1 case. Because one()/opt() are sync (no cx), the confirmation must happen in the async query_one/query_opt path (fetch-ahead to >=2 rows or end-of-data) before the cardinality check. Add a regression test (ignored live test for LONG + an offline scripted-transport test forcing more_rows=true on a single row).

Severity P1: query_one is a core 1.0 API (four-family cardinality helper); must be fixed before 1.0. NOT yet fixed (E1.2 worked around it with collect for LONG/LONG RAW).
close_reason: FIXED. query_one/query_opt now fetch ahead (private materialize_for_cardinality, <=1 extra round trip, only when one row in hand with more_rows set) before the cardinality check, so a single-row LONG/LONG RAW result returns the row instead of TooManyRows. Preserves the LONG/LOB continuation key (clones row1, keeps original in batch for next_batch). Live LONG + LONG RAW query_one regressions added to live_typed.rs; clippy + workspace + live green. Direct Rows::one()/opt() on an arraysize=1 stream keep materialized-batch semantics (noted for W4-T1).

title: [bug] Query::stream_lobs() over CLOB fails with Protocol(TtcDecode invalid ub8 length)
description: FOUND BY W3-E7.4 live e2e suite (scenario lob_stream_lobs_locator_mode, currently SKIPPED). Query::stream_lobs() (LOB locator streaming mode, opt out of default materialization) over a CLOB column returns Protocol(TtcDecode("invalid ub8 length")). Default Query LOB materialization (read/write via locator) works; only the stream_lobs() path over CLOB fails. Likely a ub8 length decode mismatch in the streamed-LOB define/fetch path for character LOBs. Needs investigation (compare BLOB vs CLOB stream_lobs decode; check the ub8 length field framing for CS_FORM_IMPLICIT). Add a regression once root-caused. To triage in E8/W4-T1.
close_reason: FIXED. LOB column decoder unconditionally read size(ub8)+chunk_size(ub4); those fields exist only in LOB-prefetch (define-fetch) responses, so a plain stream_lobs() locator fetch misaligned read_ub8 onto the locator length prefix (0x72) -> invalid ub8 length. Fix: LobDecodeMode enum (PlainLocator vs DefineMetadata) + per-cursor lob_prefetch_cursors tracking selects the correct shape; BFILE always locator-only. Offline two-shape protocol regression + live e2e lob_stream_lobs_locator_mode re-enabled. Gates + conformance (0 regressions/0 missing) green. Default LOB materialization unchanged.

title: [bug] execute_many RETURNING: BatchOutcome.rows_for exposes only first iteration's value
description: FOUND BY W3-E7.4 live e2e suite. execute_many_with over an array UPDATE ... RETURNING name INTO :3 affecting 2 rows: rows_affected=2, per_row_counts=[1,1], returning().len()=2, but returning().rows_for(2) exposes only 1 value (expected 2). ROOT CAUSE: array DML decodes RETURNING once per iteration (parse_returning_row_data, fetch.rs:869/897 pushes one (bind_index,rows) group per iteration), so return_values=[(2,[v1]),(2,[v2])]. ReturningRows.rows_for(bind_index) uses find_map -> returns only the FIRST matching group. The protocol per-iteration grouping is correct + matches python-oracledb (keep it; pyshim/conformance depends on it). FIX (driver only): BatchOutcome::from_query_result now uses ReturningRows::coalesced() which merges groups sharing a bind index in input order, so rows_for(bind_index) returns every affected row's value. ExecuteOutcome (single execute) already arrives as one group/bind, unchanged. Offline unit regression batch_outcome_coalesces_array_dml_returning_per_bind + live e2e LONG/RETURNING. Conformance unaffected (protocol unchanged).
close_reason: FIXED. BatchOutcome::from_query_result now uses ReturningRows::coalesced() merging per-iteration RETURNING groups that share a bind index, so rows_for(bind) returns every affected row's value. Protocol per-iteration grouping kept (conformance/pyshim unaffected). Offline regression batch_outcome_coalesces_array_dml_returning_per_bind + live e2e (6/6 green).

title: BUG: thin decoder mis-frames multi-packet WIDE-row results past ~1500 rows (encoded NUMBER too long / truncated TTC payload)
close_reason: Fixed in commit 3cdc451 (branch fix-widerow). Root cause: read_data_response_boundary (crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs) terminated multi-packet reassembly on an UNGUARDED trailing-byte check -- any DATA packet whose post-flags payload merely ended in TNS_MSG_TYPE_END_OF_RESPONSE (29/0x1d) or TNS_MSG_TYPE_FLUSH_OUT_BINDS (19/0x13) was treated as end-of-response, truncating wide multi-packet results. Reference impl/thin/packet.pyx::has_end_of_response guards the 0x1d check on packet_size == HEADER+3 (lone marker byte). Fix: pure data_packet_ends_response() ends the response only on the END_OF_RESPONSE/EOF data flag or a minimal lone-marker packet; flush-out-binds detected from the terminal byte of the reassembled stream. Regression: 3 hermetic unit tests (lib.rs) + live wide_row_multipacket.rs (3000x 20-col rows over 512B SDU) -- all FAIL before / PASS after. Parity sentinels green (1100 57p/5s, 2200 39p, 4300 73p, 8000 82p).

title: Fix TZ-aware TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE binds losing offset (python-oracledb #374)
description: Confirmed (adversarially verified) defect inherited from python-oracledb thin mode (their #374, still OPEN upstream). Our TIMESTAMP_TZ encoder crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin/codecs.rs:57-78 unconditionally writes the fixed (20,60)=+00:00 marker and takes no offset argument; BindValue::Timestamp (crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin/types.rs:509-518) has no field to carry a UTC offset. Result: a timezone-aware datetime bound to a TSTZ column is stored as if UTC (wall-clock kept, offset dropped) — the instant is misrepresented. FIX: add a signed offset_minutes field to BindValue::Timestamp (default 0 for naive/LTZ/plain), thread the tz offset through ToSql for tz-aware chrono types, and have encode_oracle_timestamp_tz write buf[11]=TZ_HOUR_OFFSET+offset_hours, buf[12]=TZ_MINUTE_OFFSET+offset_mins. Add a round-trip test (bind +05:30, read back, assert instant preserved). NOTE: fixing this is a differentiator — python-oracledb still has the bug.
close_reason: NOT A BUG (verified). python-oracledb #374 is a duck-typing silent-corruption: passing a tz-aware datetime, the offset is ignored. In our STATICALLY-typed API this is structurally impossible: there is NO ToSql for any tz-aware type (FixedOffset/Utc/Local — grep-empty across crates), and ToSql for NaiveDateTime always emits ora_type_num=180 (DB_TYPE_TIMESTAMP), never TSTZ (181), so encode_oracle_timestamp_tz is never reached for a value bind. A user cannot supply an offset for us to drop. This is a MISSING FEATURE (tz-aware datetime bind support), not a correctness bug. Filed nothing to fix; tz-aware bind support can be a future enhancement if desired.

title: [bug] dirty-wire: read_lob/commit/rollback/ping/AQ/change_password/subscribe skip ensure_clean_before_request after dropped future

FIX: audit all request-sending methods + add ensure_clean_before_request. ScriptedTransport regression.

title: Investigate repeated named-bind under-binding vs python-oracledb per-occurrence (adjacent to #422)
description: While confirming we do NOT reproduce python-oracledb #422 (ORA-00979), the sweep flagged a divergence: python-oracledb thin's StatementParser adds a BindInfo per placeholder OCCURRENCE (statement.pyx) and binds positionally, so a name repeated N times sends N slots. Our crate (sql_convert.rs order_named_binds) emits each named value ONCE per unique name in first-appearance order. This avoids #422 but may under-bind when a :name repeats in the SQL. VERIFY: does the server accept once-per-name for repeated placeholders, or do we need per-occurrence expansion? Add a test: 'select :a, :a from dual' with named bind :a -> assert correct result / no ORA error. If broken, expand named binds per-occurrence.
close_reason: NOT A BUG (verified LIVE against Oracle Free 23ai). Hypothesis: order_named_binds (once per distinct name) under-binds repeated SQL placeholders vs python-oracledb thin's per-occurrence (statement.pyx:343). DISPROVEN: live repro query_named('select :v + :v from dual', :v=5) returns 10, not ORA-01008 — Oracle binds the repeated placeholder BY NAME, so once-per-distinct-name is correct AND sidesteps #422 (per-occurrence optimizer confusion). Added regression test crates/oracledb/tests/repro_repeated_named_bind.rs.

comments.author: durakovic

Built a CONTAINER ROUND-TRIP differential (harness/differential/diff_oracle.py): server encodes, both rust-oracledb (via oracledb_pyshim thin_impl swap) and reference python-oracledb thin decode the SAME wire bytes; fetched Python values asserted identical. title: [ROAD] rust-oracledb → 1.0: correctness-hardened, contract-stable thin Oracle driver
description:
# Road to 1.0 — program epic

**North Star:** ship a correctness-hardened, diagnosable, resource-bounded pure-Rust async thin-mode Oracle driver. `oraclemcp` is the primary consumer (ships as a single static binary), but the crate is publicly usable and carries a FULL SemVer + support contract. 1.0 is a maturity AND compatibility milestone.

Umbrella for the whole program, split into 5 waves (child epics): W0 Evidence & CI · W1 Architecture · W2 0.3.0 migration · W3 Qualification · W4 Freeze & release. Source plan: docs/ROAD_TO_1_0.md (+ docs/API_DESIGN.md for the W1-T3 public API). These beads are meant to be self-contained — you should not need the markdown.

## Binding decisions (ADRs; files written by W0-T1)
- ADR-0001 — Keep asupersync + the pinned nightly (nightly-2026-05-11) through 1.0. oracledb is oraclemcp's engine; oraclemcp ships one static binary, so nightly is build-time-only and invisible to its users. asupersync is an asset (cancel-correct; LabRuntime+DPOR reach a bug class the single-threaded python suite cannot). Review triggers: try_trait_v2 stabilizes; an equivalent stable path appears; the pin blocks a security update; external Rust-crate adoption becomes a product goal.
- ADR-0002 — Full external SemVer + support contract; blocking cargo-semver-checks from 0.3.0 (decision (b)). Breaking cleanup happens in a 0.3.0 migration release. semver-checks runs ADVISORY during 0.3.0 dev (so the redesign isn't blocked) and flips to BLOCKING the moment 0.3.0 ships (0.3.0 = baseline), staying blocking. The gate guards UNINTENDED breaks only; intentional breaks are allowed but take the correct bump (0.x: a break is a MINOR bump, 0.3.x→0.4.0; patch must be non-breaking) + a baseline refresh in the same release. Rationale: a driver is a bounded domain — once parity+extras land and the API is designed deliberately pre-0.3.0, growth is overwhelmingly additive (#[non_exhaustive] value enums + as_* accessors; new methods); Oracle's backward-compatible TTC/TNS never forces an API break.
- ADR-0003 — Release evidence is per exact candidate SHA. Scheduled canary/soak lanes run `main` for DISCOVERY; the 1.0 gate is an explicit exact-SHA release-qualification run on the frozen RC, executed by the agent/operator and judged by objective evidence gates rather than subjective approval.

## Cross-cutting resolved decisions
- The connection pool is ASYNC-NATIVE with a `block_on` sync facade (mirrors the Connection/BlockingConnection model); loom is DROPPED in favour of asupersync DPOR. The old Condvar/GIL pool design existed only so the PyO3 pyshim could release the GIL; the pyshim is publish=false and must not shape the shipped driver. See W1-T7 + W3-E4.
- Five asupersync-leverage refinements are folded in, each verified against the vendored asupersync 0.3.4 source: single op-deadline (W1-T3), bounded recovery budget + checkpoint invariant (W1-T2), Outcome/CancelKind discipline (W1-T6), lab oracles-as-gates + crashpacks + chaos/VirtualTcp (W3-E3/E4).
- R10 RESOLVED: cargo-semver-checks 0.48.0 runs cleanly under the pin (196 checks each on oracledb-protocol + oracledb); gate those two LIBRARY crates only — the oracledb-derive proc-macro is skipped (guard it with trybuild). Async-bridge RESOLVED: no native PyO3↔asupersync bridge exists (asupersync 0.3.4 exposes no foreign-loop/external-waker surface); the shim stays spawn_blocking; nto won't-fixed (W0-T4).

## Out of scope (explicit)
- Group-A auth/wallet: GH #2/#3/#4/#6 — Kerberos, RADIUS/native-MFA, passwordless-external, broader wallet (beads rust-oracledb-o0b/qm4/x1p).
- The `57z` "beat-python" enhancement epic (rust-oracledb-57z) and its differentiators — post-1.0, except a winnowed correctness set via W3-E10.

## Definition of done
1.0.0 published to crates.io (W4-T4) with: the coherent four-family API + retained low-level surface (nothing lost), typed errors + connection disposition, ProtocolLimits resource bounds, the async-native pool, a BLOCKING SemVer contract live since 0.3.0, docs/SUPPORT.md matrix, SBOM + build provenance/attestations, standalone-tested .crate artifacts, and the full Wave-3 correctness evidence bundle green on the exact RC SHA.

title: [WAVE-0] Evidence & CI — inventories, ADRs, tiered CI, feature/SemVer/stable lanes, API ledger
description:
Wave 0 stands up the evidence + CI substrate every later wave consumes. Nothing here ships product behavior; it makes later work measurable, regenerable, and gate-able.

title: [W0-T2] Tiered, reusable CI (required/canary/soak/release-qualification) + TOOLCHAIN.md runbook
description:
WHY: CI today is only push/PR to main (no schedule/workflow_dispatch except release.yml). The program needs DISCOVERY lanes (find toolchain breaks + accumulate fuzz/property evidence on moving commits) separated from a single QUALIFYING gate (ADR-0003: exact-SHA). One reusable workflow keeps the four tiers running the SAME commands at different budgets so they can't drift.

SCOPE: a reusable _quality.yml (workflow_call; inputs: profile, budget, candidate_sha) invoked by four tiers — required.yml (PR/push deterministic checks = current gates), canary.yml (daily at a NON-ZERO minute; floating-nightly early-warning per ADR-0001 + moderate fuzz/property budgets), soak.yml (weekly deep, sharded fuzz/property/model/perf), release-qualification.yml (MANUAL, exact-SHA, full gate). Plus docs/TOOLCHAIN.md: the canary DETECTS a nightly break, this runbook is the RESPONSE (why nightly, how the pin is chosen, exact re-pin steps).

KEY CORRECTNESS FIXES (from review): evidence jobs FAIL normally — non-blocking is achieved via branch-protection SELECTION, NOT continue-on-error (which makes a job "pass" and defeats a later if:failure()). A separate if:always() reporter inspects needs.*.result, runs only on schedule/manual, and creates/updates ONE fingerprint-deduplicated issue. Default permissions: contents:read; grant issues:write/attestation only to reporter/release jobs; pin third-party actions to full SHAs; set timeout-minutes + concurrency; avoid cron at minute 0.

ACCEPTANCE: the four tiers run the same commands at different budgets; a forced evidence failure opens exactly ONE issue; required CI unchanged in scope; a scheduled red job does NOT silently pass; docs/TOOLCHAIN.md lets a fresh agent re-pin from it alone. Link TOOLCHAIN.md from README + the oracledb skill.
TESTS: a deliberately-failing evidence job in a test branch proves (a) it fails, (b) exactly one dedup issue opens, (c) it does not flip required CI.
DEPS: W0-T1. Feeds W4-T1 (freeze needs the CI tiers) + W4-T2 (uses release-qualification.yml with candidate_sha).
close_reason: Completed: W0-T2.1 reusable workflow, W0-T2.2 tier callers, W0-T2.3 reporter/security hardening, and W0-T2.4 TOOLCHAIN runbook are closed and pushed. title: [W0-T4] Close the nto bead off-gate (ADR-0001 rationale; NOT a 1.0 gate)
description:
WHY: rust-oracledb-nto ("Replace PyO3 async thread offload with native async thin execution path") is the sole in_progress bead. The async-bridge investigation RESOLVED it as won't-fix: there is NO native PyO3↔asupersync bridge — asupersync 0.3.4 exposes no foreign-loop/external-waker surface and pyo3-async-runtimes supports only tokio/async-std. The pyshim's PyO3 experimental-async + spawn_blocking + per-op block_on is the CORRECT design (node-oracledb-analogous). The pyshim is publish=false, so this has ZERO release impact.

SCOPE: close rust-oracledb-nto with that rationale; record it as non-evidence / off the release DAG.

title: [WAVE-1] Architecture — private seams + the permanent 1.0 public contract
description:
Wave 1 builds the private seams and the public API/contract that everything else rests on. Highest-stakes wave: the API designed here becomes a blocking SemVer contract at 0.3.0 (ADR-0002), so it must be deliberate.

Tasks: W1-T1 private transport/connector + ConnectionCore + scripted transport (unlocks cassette/DPOR/fault-matrix); W1-T2 session-recovery state machine (cancellation as a contract) incl. bounded recovery budget + checkpoint invariant; W1-T3 four-family operation API (query/execute/execute_many/register_query — see docs/API_DESIGN.md) incl. single op-deadline; W1-T4 calibrated type-evolution policy; W1-T5 ProtocolLimits; W1-T6 typed errors/disposition + Outcome/CancelKind discipline + redacted observability; W1-T7 async-native pool (sync facade); W1-T8 async↔blocking symmetry sweep; W1-T9 module/re-export coherence.

ALL of Wave 1 must land before the 0.3.0 migration (W2-T1). Architecture: async/blocking facades → operation reqs → private OperationCore → session recovery + typed errors/disposition + ProtocolLimits → private ConnectionCore → private WireTransport (TCP/TLS · Recording · Replay · Scripted). Pool: async pool core (acquire(&Cx).await · yielding waiter · region-owned reaper) → pure PoolState → effects, with a block_on blocking facade.
close_reason: WAVE 1 COMPLETE. All architecture tasks closed: W1-T1 (private transport/ConnectionCore), W1-T2 (session-recovery state machine + bounded recovery + checkpoints), W1-T3 (four-family API), W1-T4 (type-evolution policy), W1-T5 (ProtocolLimits), W1-T6 (typed errors/disposition + redacted observability), W1-T7 (async-native pool — reaper converted to async region task after adversarial verification caught the incomplete original + a real EDEADLK), W1-T8 (async/blocking symmetry), W1-T9 (module coherence + prelude), W1-T10 (pure PoolState). The private seams + permanent 1.0 public contract are in place.

title: [W1-T1] Private transport / connector + ConnectionCore + scripted transport
description:
WHY: One private seam beneath the public Connection lets PROD and ALL deterministic tests share the same framing/state — no drift, no permanent public test surface. The scripted transport is THE capability that unlocks cassette replay (W3-E6), DPOR (W3-E3/E4), and the fault matrix (W3-E5). Supersedes the v2 idea of public Mock variants / connect_over (resolves risks R6/R7).

SCOPE: introduce private WireTransport + Connector contracts beneath the public Connection; route production TCP/TLS, recording, strict replay, and a SCRIPTED transport through one ConnectionCore<T>. The scripted transport injects short read/write, Poll::Pending, EOF, errors, exact expected-writes, and virtual-time advances. Keep deterministic harnesses CRATE-LOCAL (they access private seams); do NOT publish a connect_over/Mock public surface. Make the accidental transport-half exports private during 0.3 (apply the W0-T5 ledger disposition: OracleReadHalf/OracleWriteHalf transport.rs:40/58).

ACCEPTANCE: a ConnectionCore runs connect/execute/fetch over a scripted transport, crate-local, zero sockets, no new public API.
TESTS: a crate-local test that drives connect→execute→fetch entirely over the scripted transport with zero sockets; assert exact expected-writes.
DEPS: W0-T5 (ledger dispositions). Foundational — feeds W1-T2, W1-T5, W1-T7, and W3-E3/E5/E6.
close_reason: Completed: W1-T1.1 through W1-T1.4 are closed. title: [W1-T10] Pure PoolState lifecycle model (deterministic, NO async deps — parallelizable)
description: WHY (Codex + self review, P2): the plan explicitly says the pure PoolState model 'has no deps and can land first', but as a subtask of W1-T7 it was gated behind W1-T2, losing that parallelism. Split out here as a STANDALONE, dependency-free task so a swarm can build + test it from day one. This is the precise oracle that shrinks the later DPOR model (W3-E4). title: [W1-T2] Session-recovery state machine (cancellation as a contract)
description:
WHY: The public contract must become "every completed op leaves the session reusable or dead." This is also the DPOR invariant (W3-E3) and exactly what oraclemcp needs. Supersedes the v2 "make drain public" note (drain stays private on both surfaces).

SCOPE:
1. An explicit internal machine: Ready → InFlight → BreakSent → Draining → Ready|Dead. Only Ready may start an op; each response belongs to exactly one op; drain is at-most-once; close is idempotent. BREAK/RESET/drain stay PRIVATE (do not expose drain_cancel_response). CancelHandle only REQUESTS cancellation; the op-completion path reconciles the wire and returns the session Ready or Dead.
2. BOUNDED RECOVERY BUDGET (pairs with the single op-deadline, W1-T3 / API_DESIGN.md principle 7): the BREAK→Draining cleanup runs under its OWN fresh, bounded recovery deadline, independent of the op's already-expired deadline — otherwise the very timeout/cancel that triggered recovery would instantly cancel the drain and force Dead on a recoverable session. Only a SECOND failure during the bounded drain yields Dead. MECHANISM NOTE (verified against asupersync 0.3.4): Cx::masked (cx/cx.rs:2238) masks only a SYNCHRONOUS finalize closure (mask_depth/MaskGuard, invariant inv.cancel.mask_bounded), so the ASYNC drain is protected by a fresh recovery sub-context/deadline, NOT by wrapping the await in masked. (Today recovery re-arms a fresh relative timeout_ms — recover_from_call_timeout(cx, timeout_ms), lib.rs:1926; the single-deadline move makes the explicit bounded recovery budget NECESSARY, not optional.)
3. CANCELLATION-OBSERVABILITY INVARIANT: every multi-round-trip loop (fetch-batch continuation, LOB chunk, recovery drain, retry) carries a cx.checkpoint() so a pending cancel is observed BETWEEN round-trips. Checkpoints already exist pervasively (~20+ sites incl. the fetch region lib.rs:3081/3134/3195/3229); this CODIFIES it as a contract that the W3-E3 DPOR cancel paths verify — it is NOT "add missing checkpoints."

ACCEPTANCE: the machine is the single owner of recovery; property/DPOR tests assert the ready-or-dead invariant across success/timeout/cancel/error/dropped-future; a recoverable post-timeout drain yields Ready (not Dead).
TESTS: property + DPOR (W3-E3) over the five wire/cancel paths; a unit test that a second-timeout-during-drain yields Dead while a recoverable drain yields Ready.
DEPS: W1-T1. Feeds W1-T3 (deadline), W1-T6 (disposition), W1-T7 (return-to-pool), W1-T8 (cancel symmetry), W3-E3.
close_reason: Completed: W1-T2.1 through W1-T2.4 are closed with the private SessionRecoveryPhase owner for Ready/InFlight/BreakSent/Draining/Dead transitions, bounded no-ambient recovery after expired caller contexts, codified cancellation checkpoints between round trips, and request-only async CancelHandle cancellation with owner-side Ready/Dead reconciliation and duplicate BREAK suppression. Child work passed focused tests plus workspace fmt, clippy, tests, diff check, and dependency-cycle gates.

title: [W1-T4] Type-evolution policy (calibrated, not blanket #[non_exhaustive])
description:
WHY: Blanket non_exhaustive makes additions POSSIBLE but imposes immediate caller costs; accessor/builder APIs are more usable for 1.x. Calibrated to effort (oraclemcp is the consumer): accessor-ize the genuinely-evolvable types; don't gold-plate fixed ones. This is what makes the blocking SemVer contract (ADR-0002) survivable — future Oracle types/fields land additively.

SCOPE (by type class, during 0.3):
- options/config (ConnectOptions, ExecuteOptions-derived request types, PoolConfig, ArrowFetchOptions): private fields + builders + getters.
- results/metadata (Rows/ExecuteOutcome/BatchOutcome, ColumnMetadata, DecodedObject, DbmsOutput, BatchServerError): accessor APIs so representation can change without caller destructuring.
- open enums (Error :679, SessionlessError :890, PoolError, SodaError, ConversionError, ArrowConversionError, NotificationOutcome, ProtocolError, SqlError, wallet/DN errors): selective #[non_exhaustive].
- value enums (BindValue types.rs:444, QueryValue/QueryValueRef): add stable as_*/conversion/visitor APIs BEFORE #[non_exhaustive] so callers aren't forced into fragile variant matches.
- fixed wire-image structs: keep internal or document as fixed.

ACCEPTANCE: an OUT-OF-WORKSPACE fixture proves construct/match/additive-evolution; adding a field/variant is non-breaking for supported types. (The pyshim is in-workspace so unaffected; the additivity scratch test MUST be out-of-workspace to be meaningful.)
TESTS: the out-of-workspace fixture crate that constructs/matches the types and still compiles after a simulated field/variant addition.
DEPS: W0-T5 (ledger), W1-T3 (the types). Feeds W2-T1.
close_reason: Type-evolution policy complete across all children: W1-T4.1 (options/config private fields+builders), W1-T4.2 (results/metadata accessors), W1-T4.3 (open-enum #[non_exhaustive]), W1-T4.4 (value-enum accessors-before-non_exhaustive), W1-T4.5 (out-of-workspace additivity fixture, independently built green). title: [W1-T6] Structured errors, recovery hints, Outcome/CancelKind discipline, redacted observability
description:
WHY: Promoted from optional differentiator to FOUNDATIONAL — it is exactly what oraclemcp needs and pairs with W1-T2. Callers must classify + decide disposition via METHODS, never by parsing display strings.

SCOPE:

title: [W1-T7] Async-native connection pool (sync facade) — loom dropped, DPOR covers it
description:
DECISION (binding): the pool is ASYNC-NATIVE; sync is a block_on facade. Today pool.rs is SYNCHRONOUS — Mutex<PoolState> + Condvar waiters + a blocking reaper std::thread, with acquire a sync fn (pool.rs:234) returning a u64 handle from a low-level PoolEngine (zero async fn). That Condvar/"no foreign locks held" design exists ONLY so the PyO3 pyshim can release the GIL before blocking. The pyshim is publish=false and MUST NOT dictate the shipped driver's architecture. Every other part of the driver is "async core + thin block_on sync facade" (Connection/BlockingConnection); the pool is the lone inversion, which forces an async consumer (e.g. oraclemcp on asupersync) to BLOCK a runtime worker on a Condvar — the canonical "blocking call inside a cooperative runtime" anti-pattern. (There is no GIL in Rust; the GIL constraint was never the driver's — only the test shim's.)

SCOPE (8 points):
1. Async core: acquire(&self, cx:&Cx, opts:AcquireOptions) -> Result<PooledConnection,PoolError>. Fast path takes an idle conn; slow path registers in an ORDERED waiter queue and .awaits a YIELDING wakeup (asupersync Notify/semaphore — NO Condvar, NO parked OS thread). Acquire timeout = a bounded deadline/Budget on the wait; cx.checkpoint() rides the wait.
2. Cancel-safety (a DPOR invariant): if the acquire future is dropped in the hand-off race window, the waiter removes itself and any granted connection is RETURNED to the pool — never leaked, never double-handed.
3. Sync facade = block_on(acquire), exposed like BlockingConnection mirrors Connection (a Blocking* surface, NOT an _blocking suffix); final naming per W1-T8. Mirror close/drain/stats/release.
4. Region-owned reaper: close(&Cx).await cancels+joins it deterministically; a bare drop lets the owning region ABORT it (no await in Drop — R11), so it never leaks. Runs open/ping/close as async effects OUTSIDE the state lock, fed back as events.
5. Pure PoolState retained: states Opening/Idle/CheckedOut/Validating/Retiring/Closing/Closed; counts DERIVED from state; effects as events; specified waiter ordering / acquire-cancel / reap / graceful-vs-force close. Now behind an async mutex + yielding queue; concurrency explored by DPOR (W3-E4), not loom.
6. PooledConnection guard + the Drop problem: async checkin CANNOT run in Drop, so drop ENQUEUES a non-blocking "return" event reconciled by the next acquire / the reaper, with a bounded sync fallback; Drop never blocks or spawns (R11). An explicit release(&Cx).await is offered. Returned conns reconciled to Ready (W1-T2) or retired.
7. Visibility: PoolEngine<B> (W0-T5 leak candidate, pool.rs:164) → pub(crate); public surface is the async Pool + its sync facade.
8. pyshim keeps driving the BLOCKING facade; its GIL handling stays the pyshim's concern (it already does spawn_blocking). No Condvar/GIL rationale survives in the shipped crate.

ACCEPTANCE: async acquire YIELDS under contention (no OS thread parked — observable in the DPOR/lab schedule); a cancel mid-acquire leaks/duplicates no connection; the sync facade is exactly block_on of the async path; the reaper is joined on close(&Cx).await and aborted (not leaked) on bare drop; the pure PoolState has full-lifecycle deterministic tests with derived counts; harness/run.sh diff stays green.
TESTS: deterministic unit tests over the pure PoolState lifecycle; W3-E4 DPOR for the concurrency invariants (no double-handout/lost-wakeup/leaked-waiter/returned-retired/idempotent-close/acquire-cancel-returns).
DEPS: W1-T1 (mint/ping Connections via ConnectionCore), W1-T2 (return-to-pool disposition). The pure PoolState sub-model has no deps and can land first. Feeds W3-E4, and (via W1-T8) the pool symmetry.

W1-T7 acceptance now met — async acquire yields (no parked thread), the reaper is an async region task (no Condvar/std::thread in the production path), close(&Cx).await cooperatively async-joins it (no executor block), drop aborts without leaking. Independently verified + adversarially read.

description:
Wave 3 builds the correctness arsenal that pushes confidence BEYOND python-oracledb's own thin test suite, then certifies it. Evidence rules: fixed seeds in required CI + rotating seeds in canary/soak; record tool versions, bounds (CPU/time/state), corpus hashes, target manifests; severity triage (no open P0/P1, no untriaged finding, P2 needs a fix or a tracked technical exception with rationale); call a model \"exhaustive\" ONLY when its finite queue empties under recorded bounds, else \"bounded clean.\"

Tasks: E1 FromSql/ToSql property round-trips; E2 manifest-driven fuzzing (+9 targets); E3 DPOR over wire/cancel paths; E4 DPOR over the async pool (loom dropped); E5 deterministic fault/fragmentation matrix; E6 secure full-connection cassette replay; E7 live support matrix + direct oraclemcp contract suite; E8 multi-pass bug-hunt sweeps; E9 perf/resource regression gates; E10 post-1.0 idea-wizard backlog (demoted).

CRITICAL: Wave-3 development runs are DISCOVERY (on moving commits). The single QUALIFYING run that must be green on one exact SHA is W4-T2 on the frozen RC (ADR-0003). Wave 3 builds the suites; W4-T2 certifies them together.

title: [W3-E3] DPOR over the wire/cancel paths (asupersync lab)
description:
WHY: asupersync's LabRuntime+DPOR reaches a concurrency/cancel bug class the single-threaded python suite cannot. Runs on W1-T1's scripted transport + W1-T2's state machine (no separate seam needed).

VERIFIED 0.3.4 API (skill docs describe a newer tree): LabRuntime/LabConfig (asupersync src/lib.rs:402), DporExplorer/ScheduleExplorer/ExplorationReport (src/lab/explorer.rs), CoverageMetrics::is_saturated/discovery_rate (:165/156); test_utils::run_test is NOT the lab.

PATHS (verified crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs): P1 timeout->BREAK->drain->reuse (:6077/6234); P2 cancel/drop-cancel auto-drain (:6198/3003); P3 shared write-mutex vs CancelHandle (:5922 — REQUIRES the async CancelHandle::cancel(&Cx) variant built in W1-T2.4); P4 close-cursors piggyback vs in_use_cursors (:1246/1255, ScheduleExplorer); P5 speculative prefetch drop (:3171).

STOPPING: P1/P2/P4/P5 exhaustive (queue empties under recorded max_runs, 0 violations); P3 bounded (is_saturated(512) && discovery_rate<0.005 — a HEURISTIC, not an exhaustiveness proof; asupersync's documented guarantee is Optimal-DPOR EFFICIENCY, not saturation=exhaustiveness).

ORACLES AS HARD GATES (verified 0.3.4): set LabConfig panic_on_futurelock + panic_on_leak + futurelock_max_idle_steps (lab/config.rs) plus the QUIESCENCE oracle (lab/crashpack/oracle.rs), so a futurelock / obligation-leak / non-quiescent-shutdown violation FAILS THE RUN, not just a summary line; and test-assert the W1-T2 ready-or-dead contract + loser-drain (cancelled losers drain their cursor; cf. lab/atp_path/harness.rs). On any failure emit a crashpack (CrashPack, lab/crashpack/...) + repro_manifest (lab/replay.rs) + write_json_summary() (lab/explorer.rs:335) + reproducer seed (one-command replay). Layer deterministic chaos onto the cancel paths — with_light_chaos() in canary / with_heavy_chaos() in soak (lab/chaos.rs:49/53) over a VirtualTcpStream/VirtualNet surface (net/tcp/virtual_tcp.rs, lab/network/harness.rs).

ACCEPTANCE: all paths clean under recorded bounds; oracles green as gates; crashpack + seed captured on any failure.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the DPOR model).
DEPS: W1-T2 (state machine; subsumes W1-T1) + W1-T2.4 (async cancel handle for P3). Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: DPOR wire/cancel: in-crate harness over scripted-transport seam; bounded operation-vs-interrupt exploration replaying real ConnectionCore wire recovery for each seed (full-ConnectionCore-in-LabRuntime fallback, documented). Verified UserCancel maps to Error::Cancelled, Timeout to CallTimeout, recovery ends Ready, exactly one BREAK + one RESET. Guards the W1-T6.2 cancel-checkpoint anti-pattern.

title: [W3-E4] DPOR over the async pool (shares the E3 lab; loom dropped)
description:
WHY: With W1-T7 the pool is ASYNC-NATIVE on asupersync sync primitives, so its interleavings are visible to the SAME DPOR/LabRuntime machinery as the wire/cancel paths (E3) — and LOOM IS REMOVED from the plan. Rationale: loom intercepts std/loom sync + atomics, whereas DPOR explores async task/await/obligation interleavings; once the pool holds no std::sync/atomics concurrency island, loom has nothing left to model. Guard: if any std-sync island DOES remain after W1-T7, loom covers ONLY that, reported bounded clean; otherwise loom is not a dependency.

SCOPE: model the W1-T7 pure PoolState shell + yielding waiter queue with a mock PoolBackend (pool.rs:90-109) under DPOR. INVARIANTS: no double-handout, no lost wakeup, no leaked waiter, no returned-retired connection, idempotent close, AND acquire-cancel returns (never leaks/duplicates) its connection (W1-T7). STOPPING: finite queue empties under recorded max_runs → exhaustive; else bounded clean (is_saturated/discovery_rate is a heuristic, not a completeness proof — §7). Oracles as hard gates + crashpack / repro_manifest / write_json_summary() + reproducer seed on any failure, exactly as E3.

ACCEPTANCE: all invariants hold under recorded bounds; oracles green as gates; artifacts captured on any failure.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the pool DPOR model).
DEPS: W1-T7. Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: DPOR pool: exhaustive depth-7 lifecycle enumeration (3125 sequences) + async DPOR fallback. Found and FIXED a real bug (pool.rs:467): force-close racing in-flight open/ping failure requeued a waiter into a closed pool. Fix suppresses waiter requeue while closing. Invariants verified: no missed wakeup, FIFO, no double-handout, force-close drains waiters. Gates + conformance (0 regressions) green.

title: [W3-E5] Deterministic fault & fragmentation matrix
description:
WHY: On W1-T1's scripted transport, prove the decoder is invariant to BYTE SPLITS and robust to faults at every protocol phase — without a live DB. This is the metamorphic backbone of the sans-io layer.

SCOPE: generate fault points from protocol phases (connect/auth/execute/fetch/LOB/cancel/close) and inject short read/write, Pending, EOF, I/O error, timeout, virtual-time advances; assert FRAGMENTATION INVARIANCE (every legal byte split → same semantic result); exercise faults around BREAK/RESET (next op never consumes the previous response). Run the fragmentation-invariance property against the PURE decoder (proptest over arbitrary split points), not only through the async transport, so the sans-io layer carries its own metamorphic guarantee independent of any runtime.

ACCEPTANCE: a generated phase/fault coverage report; every case ends Ready/Dead/Closed; no live DB.
TESTS: this task IS tests (the fault/fragmentation matrix + the pure-decoder split-invariance property).
DEPS: W2-T1 (stabilized types). Feeds W3-E8.
close_reason: E5 deterministic fault and fragmentation matrix complete: fragmentation_invariance.rs (split-invariance, committed 600ec18) + fault_injection.rs (fault-point generator, all phases). Decoders proven robust under corruption; no bug found.

title: perf: borrowed fast-path only snapshots last row (stop materializing every cell to owned) lib.rs:5739
description: DEFERRED (reverted, kept correct-by-reasoning). The borrowed for_each_row_ref paging loop (lib.rs ~5743) materializes EVERY row to owned just to seed the next page's duplicate-column resolution; only the LAST row is ever used as the seed. A row_count()-gated 'materialize only the last row' is correct by reasoning [row_count()==row_starts.len()==the for_each iteration count, so last_owned == the last row, identical seed]. BLOCKER: the only live test exercising the exact concern [cross-page bit-vector seed] is borrowed_fetch_handles_multipage_clob_lob_prefetch, which is #[ignore] AND currently environment-flaky [fails on clean HEAD too — see the new sibling bead]. Per no-unverified-changes, NOT shipped until a DETERMINISTIC regression exists [e.g. a non-LOB query with a constant column forcing bit-vector duplication across a small arraysize, run via for_each_row_ref] AND a clean container. perf2+perf3 shipped separately.
close_reason: RE-APPLIED + VERIFIED now that bur7 unblocked the CLOB seed test. for_each_row_ref now snapshots ONLY the last row of each page as the next-page duplicate-column seed [row_count()==row_starts.len()==the for_each iteration count, so last_owned is the same last row the old overwrite-every-row logic produced], instead of materializing every borrowed row to owned. Verified: live_borrowed_fetch 2/2 across 3 deterministic runs [the duplicate-CLOB compression test is the exact seed verifier — it exercises cross-page bit-vector resolution]; clippy/fmt/gen_baseline[public_api unchanged] green. The stranded_prefetch test failure is a SEPARATE pre-existing #[ignore] issue [fails without perf1 too; does not use for_each_row_ref] tracked separately.

title: [CONF-GAP] Close 103 pre-existing python-oracledb conformance gaps (4 root causes)
description:
DISCOVERED via the first CLEAN full differential conformance run (2026-06-21, 2578/2578 tests, env fixed). 103 tests pass in python-oracledb (baseline) but fail in our driver. PROVEN PRE-EXISTING / NOT a Wave-2 regression: validate_bind_rows_shape + the dataframe/Arrow + protocol paths are git-unchanged by Wave-2; old methods and the new execute_raw both funnel through the SAME execute_query_with_bind_rows_and_options_core (lib.rs:5196), so the migration is conformance-neutral (stash/static-proven). These are correctness-to-convergence gaps to close before 1.0.

ROOT CAUSES (counts from the clean run):
 1) 66 bind-shape validation too strict (crates/oracledb/src/sql_convert.rs validate_bind_rows_shape, gated only by !scroll_operation at lib.rs:5195):
    - 14 parse-only/no-binds: cursor.parse(sql) with placeholders + 0 binds. python-oracledb parse() DESCRIBES without binding; our core validates bind count on parse_only -> rejects. FIX CANDIDATE: also skip validation when exec_options.parse_only() (mirror the scroll_operation skip). e.g. test_5416, test_4311, test_4324, test_1614, test_6308.
    - 52 partial-bind mismatch: execute with fewer binds than placeholders (e.g. 'expects 3 but 1 supplied'). python-oracledb defers the bind-count error to the SERVER (ORA-01008) or allows it; our client-side validation rejects with a different message. e.g. test_8616, test_3916, test_4216.
 2) 24 'protocol resource limit exceeded' (oracledb.exceptions.DatabaseError) in the DataFrame/Arrow fetch path (fetch_df_all / fetch_df_batches). e.g. test_9700-range, test_8048/9324 families. Investigate the resource-limit/protocol cap in the Arrow/dataframe fetch.
 3) 12 error-code mismatch: driver raises a different DPY-/ORA- code than the reference expects. e.g. test_9714.
 4) 1 expected-error-not-raised: test_5528 expects DPY-4005 (pool) not raised.

ACCEPTANCE: harness/run.sh diff shows 0 regressions (or each remaining one is explicitly justified + filtered with rationale). Add unit/e2e coverage for each fix. Gates green.

SUGGEST: granularize into 4 child beads (one per root cause) when scheduled. This BLOCKS a clean 1.0 (W4-T2 RC qualification needs 0-regression conformance). DEPS: discovered-from W2-T1.2 conformance run.
notes: ALL 3 ROOT CAUSES FIXED (pending final full-conformance confirmation): bind-validation 66 (validate_bind_rows_shape rectangular-only), direct-path-load 36 (removed misused check_batch_rows(batch_size)), pool DPY-4005 1 (asupersync wheel-timer bug — short timed-wait timer fires only on 1st use/process; fixed via TimedAcquireDeadline std-thread watchdog in pool.rs + pool teardown GIL-deadlock fixed via helper-thread runtime drop). Pool fix: diagnosed by Claude teammate (deterministic repro), implemented+verified by Codex (test_5528 x5 + module 47 + test_2400 51 + gates green), adversarially reviewed by me (sound: watchdog gated on TIMEDWAIT, no leak, EDEADLK preserved, connections still closed sync). Full conformance running for 0-regression confirmation.

title: oraclemcp production-validation of 0.5.0 green-lights 1.0.0 promotion
description: The 1.x-contract code, exhaustively qualified, is shipped as 0.5.0 to crates.io for real-world validation BEFORE making the 1.0 stability promise. Gate: oraclemcp upgrades to oracledb 0.5.0 from the public registry, exercises it in production-representative use, and reports back. PASS criteria: oraclemcp builds + its suites pass against 0.5.0 from crates.io; no new driver defects surface in real use over a soak window. On PASS this becomes the production-readiness signal that, together with a fresh exact-SHA re-qualification at the 1.0 cut, green-lights the 1.0.0 promotion. Blocks rust-oracledb-road-to-1-0-llv.5.4.

title: Public structured ADT object/collection decoding API with metadata (native) (oraclemcp #9)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #9. VERIFIED current state: QueryValue::Object(Box<ObjectValue>) carries the packed pickle image (types.rs:99,171); oracledb-protocol/src/thin/dbobject.rs has decode_dbobject_text / decode_dbobject_xmltype_text (a TEXT projection used by the parity suite). The codec exists internally. GAP: no PUBLIC API to decode QueryValue::Object / QueryValue::Array into STRUCTURED Rust values with metadata — object type schema+name, attribute names/types/nullability, scalar attributes decoded with the same rules as normal columns, collection element-type metadata, bounded nested traversal, and a lossless fallback for values it cannot decode. oraclemcp emits opaque/unsupported markers today so agents don't mistake packed protocol bytes for decoded business values. ACCEPTANCE: decode simple ADT objects with scalar attributes + collections with scalar elements; explicit machine-classifiable 'unsupported' for complex/nested shapes; enough metadata for agent-facing JSON to name fields+types; BOUNDED decode so malformed/huge object payloads cannot cause unbounded memory use; live tests for a simple object type and a simple collection type.
close_reason: Implemented public structured ADT decode: describe_object_type (ALL_TYPE_ATTRS for objects, ALL_COLL_TYPES for collections) + decode_object for scalar attributes and collection elements, bounded against the image, typed UnsupportedFeature for nested/unsupported shapes. Live tests for a simple object (incl. NULL attr) and a VARRAY of NUMBER (incl. NULL element + empty). Commit 85abc4c.

title: Columnar decode straight into Arrow builders (transpose-during-parse, no QueryValue String)
notes: Dueling-wizards consensus #1 (cross-model 820). Decode fetched rows directly into per-column typed accumulators (i128+scale Decimal128, offsets+bytes, NullBuffer) during parse, skipping QueryValue row materialization on the arrow path. Python-structurally-impossible (GIL per cell). Substrate exists: borrowed fetch (0ta), decode-offload (xad), arrow.rs. CONSTRAINT: wire is row-major (fetch.rs:667/693) so this is transpose-during-parse, NOT column-major wire read (effort L-XL). Gate behind `arrow` feature; differential proptest must match build_record_batch cell-for-cell. Pairs with the inline-i128-NUMBER bead.
close_reason: Columnar-Arrow merged (perf-push 1f4d6c2): 95% fewer allocs, byte-identical

title: Broaden + document thin wallet compatibility: encrypted PEM, p12, typed format diagnostics (oraclemcp #6)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #6. VERIFIED CURRENT STATE (so the bead is honest): we DO support — unencrypted ewallet.pem (oracledb-protocol/src/tls/wallet.rs parse_ewallet_pem), cwallet.sso (experimental feature, via the internal PKCS12 parser tls/pfx.rs used only by tls/sso.rs), a wallet password through ConnectOptions (wallet_password / with_wallet_password), AND a TYPED diagnostic when the PEM key is encrypted ('wallet private key is encrypted; supply an unencrypted ewallet.pem (orapki -auto_login) or use cwallet.sso', wallet.rs:194-197). So issue #6 UNDER-states current support. REAL GAPS: (a) encrypted ewallet.pem PKCS8 private keys are NOT decrypted even when wallet_password is supplied (we error) — add decryption; (b) no standalone ewallet.p12 wallet format (pfx parser is private/internal to cwallet.sso); (c) no operator-facing documentation of the supported-format matrix. ACCEPTANCE: encrypted-wallet handling where feasible; typed/machine-classifiable errors for unsupported formats; redact wallet paths+passwords in ALL formatted output (already partially done); tests for encrypted-wallet handling + unsupported-format diagnostics; docs/ section listing ewallet.pem (unencrypted), cwallet.sso, wallet_password usage, and what each error means.

title: Async streaming row Stream / lending iterator over borrowed batches
notes: Dueling-wizards (both proposed independently, 730/660). Builds on for_each_row_ref + BorrowedRowBatch (lib.rs). HONEST framing: page-granular pull, not item-level backpressure (TTC pushes a configured array per round trip); API bifurcates into lending next_row (zero-copy) + an owned-row futures::Stream adapter. Constant-memory large-result fetch is the win. Effort L.

title: Decode offload to asupersync worker pool: the GIL-killer (split socket-read from CPU decode)
notes: Framing (B) speculative next-page prefetch, one-page look-ahead. Chosen over (A) worker-pool decode-offload: decode stays on the SAME task so borrowed QueryValueRef buffers never cross a thread/await boundary (no unsafe, no lifetime risk); reuses the existing array-fetch round trip; no channel/worker. for_each_row_ref now sends page K+1 FETCH (fetch_rows_request, send-only) before decoding page K, reads K+1 next iter (fetch_rows_ref_response). Cancellation: fetch_rows_request arms cancel_drain_pending for the whole window until consumed; a drop anywhere -> next op break+drains the stranded page (merged CancelDrainGuard+break_and_drain). MEASURED loopback: read-wait/page -5.6%..-24% (robust overlap signal), realistic-consumer wall -12.5%..-19.5%, criterion end-to-end noise-to-modest on loopback (win larger on real-network RTT). SOUND: byte-identical rows, cancellation-safe (tested + negative-control proof). PARITY EXACT: 1100 57p/5s, 2200 39p, 4300 73p, 8000_dataframe 82p (pyshim untouched). Files: crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs, tests/prefetch_overlap.rs, benches/thin_driver.rs (oracledb_prefetch), examples/profile_fetch_attribution.rs, tests/artifacts/perf/xad-*. ALSO SATISFIES bead 3oi.
close_reason: Speculative next-page prefetch (framing B) implemented + proven: read-wait -5.6%..-24% (overlap), realistic-consumer wall -12.5%..-19.5%, sound (no unsafe, cancellation-safe w/ negative-control, byte-identical), parity exact (1100/2200/4300/8000), gate green. Also satisfies 3oi.

title: [bug] borrowed fetch path ignores LOB-prefetch decode mode (CLOB/BLOB desync on page 2+ via for_each_row_ref/arrow)
description: E8 R4 (3/3). Borrowed-path TWIN of the owned jbh9 fix. parse_query_response_borrowed* hardcode LobDecodeMode::PlainLocator (fetch.rs:1690/1369/1613); owned fetch_rows selects mode from lob_prefetch_cursors (lib.rs:5686). for_each_row_ref + arrow columnar paged fetch via fetch_rows_ref_response always PlainLocator -> for a LOB-prefetch cursor the size(ub8)+chunk_size(ub4) prefix is mis-framed on page 2+ -> corruption or invalid ub8 length. FIX: additive DefineMetadata borrowed parse variant + select from lob_prefetch_cursors like owned. Ref packet.pyx read_lob_with_length.
close_reason: FIXED. Additive parse_define_fetch_response_borrowed_with_limits + fetch_rows_ref/_response select DefineMetadata from lob_prefetch_cursors (matches owned lib.rs:5781). Borrowed multi-page CLOB/BLOB no longer desyncs on page 2+. Offline borrowed-vs-owned round-trip regression + ignored live. Semver additive, conformance 0 regressions.

title: Ergonomic REF CURSOR + implicit result-set consumption API (native) (oraclemcp #8)
description: From oraclemcp GH issue #8. VERIFIED current state: the low-level pieces exist and are PUBLIC — QueryValue::Cursor{cursor_id} (protocol/thin/types.rs:93), QueryResult.implicit_resultsets: Option<Vec<QueryValue>> (types.rs:555), and Connection::{scroll_cursor, release_cursor, close_cursor, define_and_fetch_rows_with_columns}. The wire/protocol path is suite-tested via the pyshim (implicit results + REF CURSOR). GAP: no ERGONOMIC high-level native API to (a) fetch rows from a returned QueryValue::Cursor without hand-managing cursor_id, (b) enumerate+fetch implicit result sets from a PL/SQL call, (c) get per-child-cursor column metadata, (d) RAII or explicit release semantics, (e) easy row/byte caps. oraclemcp emits 'unsupported' placeholders for REF CURSOR cells today. ACCEPTANCE: consume REF CURSOR OUT values + implicit result sets through stable public APIs; column metadata per child result set; bounded fetch + deterministic cleanup/release; live tests for a PL/SQL block returning a REF CURSOR and one returning implicit result sets.
close_reason: Implemented + LIVE-VERIFIED (Oracle Free 23ai). Connection::fetch_cursor(cursor: &CursorValue, max_rows) + BlockingConnection wrapper: drains a returned, self-describing REF CURSOR (a QueryValue::Cursor OUT value OR a QueryResult.implicit_resultsets entry) WITHOUT manual cursor-id handling. Column metadata on the returned QueryResult.columns (CursorValue already carries columns). First fetch = define-fetch, then fetch_rows_with_columns continuation loop (more_rows + previous_row) bounded by max_rows, releases the cursor when done. Live tests (crates/oracledb/tests/live_ref_cursor.rs): DBMS_SQL.RETURN_RESULT implicit result set -> 1-col cursor, fetch_cursor -> [1..5]; 100-row cursor bounded to 10. oraclemcp #8: agents can now consume REF CURSOR cells via a stable public API instead of placeholders.
